developer.comment: Built and maintained by Google (google-gemini org).

status.comment: Active as of 2026-06-02, but Google announced (May 19-20, 2026) it will stop serving requests on June 18, 2026 for free, Google AI Pro, and Ultra tiers plus individual Code Assist users; enterprise (Code Assist Standard/Enterprise) customers retain access. Apache-2.0 repo stays public. A closed-source Go-rewrite successor (Antigravity CLI, `agy`) was introduced. Re-verified 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Agent itself is Apache-2.0 open source; the hosted service is freemium (free tier plus paid plans).

specialization.comment: Broad coding/agentic terminal tool; not optimized for a single niche.

interface.comment: Terminal-first command-line agent. (A separate GitHub Action exists for CI, but the product itself is a CLI.)

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal; not an IDE plugin (the related Gemini Code Assist IDE integration is a separate product).

default-model-provider.comment: Defaults to Google's Gemini models (e.g. Gemini 2.5 Pro / latest).

model-choice.comment: User can select among Gemini models via the -m flag (e.g. gemini-2.5-flash vs 2.5-pro). Choice is within Google's model family only; non-Google providers are not natively supported.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Supports a personal Gemini API key or Vertex AI credentials in addition to OAuth sign-in.

local-model-support.comment: No official local-model support (no native Ollama/LM Studio/vLLM). Only Google-hosted Gemini models; local use relies on community forks or unofficial base-URL redirection. Checked 2026-06-02.

multi-file-editing.comment: Analyzes and edits across an entire repository in one task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agentic loop chains edit/run/fix steps toward a goal (subject to its permission gating).

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands as part of its loop, with sandbox/trusted-folder controls.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP server integration; /mcp command lists configured servers.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Subagents defined via markdown + YAML frontmatter in .gemini/agents; built-in research subagents (codebase_investigator, cli_help) run in Plan Mode.

codebase-indexing.comment: Reads files on demand into context (large context window) rather than building a persistent semantic/embeddings index. No documented index store. Checked 2026-06-02.

planning-mode.comment: Documented Plan Mode (read-only plan-then-execute), expanded in 2026 with built-in research subagents.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image input (images, PDFs, sketches) per the repo feature list.

browser-computer-use.comment: Has web search/fetch (grounding) tools but does not drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify its own changes. Checked 2026-06-02.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Beyond explicit @agent invocation, the main agent autonomously delegates: its system prompt instructs it to route a task to an expert subagent when the task matches that subagent's description (model-driven automatic delegation), without the user invoking it. Subagents are exposed to the main agent as tools it can call mid-task. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational REPL with per-action approval; can run multi-step tasks autonomously. No inline tab completion and no detached cloud/async dispatch.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal chat agent; no as-you-type tab autocomplete (that is the separate Gemini Code Assist IDE product).

background-async-execution.comment: Runs interactively in the foreground terminal session; not a fire-and-forget cloud/background dispatcher. (A non-interactive scripted mode exists but requires the session to run.)

persistent-memory.comment: Hierarchical project memory from GEMINI.md files loaded across sessions; /memory command manages it.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: /restore reverts project files to the state just before a tool was executed; conversation checkpointing also supported.

git-integration.comment: Can run git commands and is git-aware via its shell tool and GitHub Action integration.

pr-creation.comment: Via the official GitHub Action / GitHub integration it can open and update pull requests; the CLI itself can also drive git/gh.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Project-level GEMINI.md context files; the contextFileName setting can also accept AGENTS.md.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensions (TypeScript modules adding custom tools/commands), custom slash commands, and MCP servers.

permission-model.comment: Prompts for approval before tool/command execution; offers sandbox and trusted-folder controls; auto-accept (YOLO) and policy settings make it configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: The CLI binary is local but depends on Google's hosted Gemini/Vertex backend; no on-prem/self-hosted model deployment. Checked 2026-06-02.

privacy-mode.comment: With Gemini Code Assist Standard/Enterprise, prompts and code are not collected or used to train models. The free individual tier may use data for training, so the zero-training guarantee is tier-dependent.

enterprise-controls.comment: Via Gemini Code Assist Enterprise on Google Cloud: SSO and RBAC through Cloud IAM, Cloud Audit Logs, and VPC Service Controls.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier (OAuth/free Code Assist), fixed-price subscriptions (Google AI Pro, Code Assist Standard/Enterprise), and pay-as-you-go via Gemini API / Vertex AI. Free/Pro/Ultra/individual access is slated to stop June 18, 2026 (enterprise retained). Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: Generous free tier (OAuth login: 60 req/min, 1,000 req/day) covering individual use. Note: Google announced this free-tier access ends June 18, 2026.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Genuine free tier covers core coding use (1,000 req/day). Cheapest paid plan is Google AI Pro at ~$19.99/mo; Code Assist Standard ~$22.80/user/mo. Free-tier access scheduled to end 2026-06-18.

maturity.comment: Polished, widely deployed GA tool (100,000+ GitHub stars, 6,000+ merged community PRs), but rated 4 (not 5) given active flux and the May 2026 announced transition off the free/individual tier toward Antigravity CLI. Checked 2026-06-02.

community-size.comment: Google-backed with rapid, broad adoption: 100,000+ GitHub stars and 6,000+ merged community PRs.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Extensions, custom commands, Agent Skills, and MCP server ecosystem; numerous community extensions and forks.

project-memory-files.comment: Default auto-loaded instruction file is GEMINI.md (a vendor-specific filename, hence `custom`). The context.fileName / contextFileName setting can be reconfigured to other names (e.g. AGENTS.md) or a list, but GEMINI.md is the out-of-the-box convention. Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: GEMINI.md is loaded hierarchically: system/enterprise (system-wide settings + system GEMINI.md, admin-controlled via GEMINI_CLI_SYSTEM_SETTINGS_PATH), user (~/.gemini/GEMINI.md), project root (./GEMINI.md, stops at the .git root), and subdirectory/folder (e.g. ./src/GEMINI.md). All four levels are concatenated.

scoped-rules-files.comment: The GEMINI.md hierarchy is always concatenated and applied (always-on); subdirectory files are location-based but not glob/file-type conditional, and @path imports modularize files rather than gate them. No Cursor-style path-glob or file-type conditional rule activation. (On-demand, agent-requested loading exists for Agent Skills, captured separately in the Skills group.) Checked 2026-06-02.

auto-memory.comment: The model can autonomously persist cross-session facts via the save_memory tool (appended to the '## Gemini Added Memories' section of ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md), e.g. when asked to 'remember' something. An experimental Auto Memory feature (settings flag experimental.autoMemory, default off, toggleable) additionally mines past sessions in the background and proposes durable memories + reusable Agent Skills — review-first via /memory inbox, nothing applied automatically. The save_memory tool can be excluded via tool settings, so the behavior can be turned off. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Ships Agent Skills: self-contained directories with a SKILL.md (metadata + instructions + bundled assets) loaded on demand. Also supports reusable custom slash commands (.toml) — both beyond the always-on GEMINI.md file. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-invocation.comment: Model-callable: the agent auto-activates a matching Agent Skill via the activate_skill tool when a task matches its description. User-callable: custom slash commands (e.g. /test, namespaced /git:commit) are explicitly invoked by the user.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: only each skill's name and description are injected into the system prompt at session start; the full SKILL.md body and assets are pulled in only when the model activates the skill.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills live in user (~/.gemini/skills/ or ~/.agents/skills/) or workspace (.gemini/skills/ or .agents/skills/) directories — installed by dropping in files (manual-files) or bundled inside Gemini CLI extensions, which install from GitHub repos via `gemini extensions install` (git). No single curated one-click skills marketplace observed. Checked 2026-06-02.

sandboxing-technology.comment: On macOS uses Apple Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) with selectable profiles; cross-platform uses Docker/Podman containers (ghcr.io/google/gemini-cli image or custom .gemini/sandbox.Dockerfile). Sandboxing is OFF by default — must be enabled via -s/--sandbox, GEMINI_SANDBOX env, or settings; otherwise tools run with the user's full privileges. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: tools.allowed / tools.core allowlist by tool name (per-tool) and by shell command prefix like run_shell_command(git) or run_shell_command(npm test) (command-prefix). The auto_edit approval mode auto-approves edit tools (write_file/replace) while still prompting for other tools (read/write distinction). When the sandbox is enabled, writes are confined to the project directory (per-path). No per-network-domain host allowlist (network is profile-level open vs proxied, not host-specific). Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: Allow (tools.allowed/tools.core) and exclude (tools.exclude, mcp.excluded) lists; for MCP tool filtering the docs state excludeTools takes precedence over includeTools — i.e. exclusion always wins. No documented specificity-based re-override (nested exceptions) or user-ordered first-match evaluation. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: User-authored allow/exclude rules plus preset approval modes (default/auto_edit/yolo/plan) in settings.json. No native programmable per-action permission callback/hook that inspects each concrete action and allow/deny/rewrites it (the extensibility surface adds tools/commands, not a policy decision point). Highest rung reached is declarative-rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No documented out-of-the-box deny patterns or built-in refusal for secrets (.env, key files); the user would have to add their own exclude/ignore rules. Sandbox write confinement (when enabled) is path-based, not secret-aware. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: tools.allowed entries in user/project .gemini/settings.json persist allowlisted tools/command prefixes across sessions; a per-tool 'Always allow for this session/workspace' option and security.enablePermanentToolApproval remember approvals durably. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: YOLO mode auto-approves all tool calls; enabled via --yolo/-y, GEMINI_YOLO_MODE=true, the approval-mode setting, or Ctrl+Y at runtime. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: Egress is profile-controlled when sandboxing is enabled: '-open' Seatbelt profiles allow unrestricted network, '-proxied' profiles route all traffic through a configured proxy/gateway. Default profile (permissive-open) allows unrestricted network, and with sandboxing off (the default) there is no network gating at all. No built-in per-domain host allowlist. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box the default approval mode prompts for confirmation on each tool call (edits and shell commands alike); auto_edit and yolo are opt-in. Checked 2026-06-12.

auto-accept-mode.comment: auto_edit approval mode auto-approves edit tools (write_file/replace) while still prompting for other tools — the middle rung between per-action prompting and full YOLO bypass. Toggled in-session by cycling approval modes with Shift+Tab (shows 'accepting edits'), or set at launch via --approval-mode auto_edit. Distinct from the separate full-bypass YOLO mode (Unsupervised Mode). Checked 2026-07-18.
